current_run_py_contract.input: self-contained python source string (with literal SR, paths, freqs interpolated as numeric/string literals via to_string())
current_run_py_contract.output: side-effect WAV file at a path the hexa caller already chose via _new_path() before invocation
current_run_py_contract.return: always true (does not propagate exec failure or python traceback)
organ_classes: sources: vocal, vocal_lf, breath, tone, chirp, silence
filters: pipe, pipe3, pipe_glide, lpf, hpf, bpf, notch, gain, envelope, reverb
analyzers/probes invoked from omega_audio_emp.hexa via the same _run_py path
current_run_py_contract.import_diversity: every code blob re-imports numpy + wave; subset adds 'from scipy.signal import {lfilter|butter|sosfilt|lfiltic|fftconvolve|resample_poly}' — these are the dominant cold-start cost

worker_startup.process: single python3 daemon spawned lazily on first _run_py() call within an anima session; binary at /Users/ghost/core/anima/anima-speak/_audio_worker.py (new file, design-only)
boot_actions: from scipy.signal import lfilter, lfiltic, butter, sosfilt, fftconvolve, resample_poly
import sys, os, json, traceback, signal, time
rng = np.random.default_rng()  # one shared RNG; seed-overridable per request
preallocate small scratch buffer (np.empty(SR * 2) — covers most calls without realloc)
worker_startup.amortization: 143ms cold start paid once per anima session; subsequent calls pay only synthesis (~5ms) + WAV IO (~5ms) + protocol overhead (~1-2ms)
worker_startup.memory_resident: ~80 MB (numpy + scipy + python interpreter); compared to 0 MB ephemeral baseline

request_protocol.transport: stdin/stdout pipes — chosen over named pipes/UDS because it survives any shell quoting hexa already does, and is killed automatically when the hexa process exits (no zombie risk on parent crash)
request_protocol.framing: newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON) — one request per line; payload itself never contains a literal newline because we base64 the python code string

schema.id: u64 monotonic, hexa-side counter — used to match response to caller for future parallelism
schema.code_b64: base64(utf-8(python_source))  // already self-contained — same string today's _run_py builds
schema.expect_path: optional — the WAV path hexa expects to be created; worker sanity-checks file_size on success path

request_protocol.why_b64_not_length_prefix: length-prefix is faster but harder to debug with `tee` / shell inspection; for a 10ms request budget the b64 cost is <0.5ms for 2-3KB payloads

response_protocol.framing: single NDJSON line per request, on stdout

schema.err: string — first 512 chars of traceback if ok=false, else ""

response_protocol.stderr_use: worker forwards uncaught traceback fragments to its own stderr (captured by hexa via 2>>logfile); stdout stays clean JSON
response_protocol.hexa_side_adapter: _run_py(code) keeps signature, but body becomes: ensure_worker(); send(code); resp = recv(); return resp.ok && file_size(resp.expect_path) >= WAV_MIN_BYTES — fully backward compatible at call sites (zero edits to the 49 organ bodies)

lifecycle.spawn_trigger: lazy — first call to _run_py() in a session checks for live worker via PID file, spawns if absent
lifecycle.ownership_model: one worker per anima process (per session). NOT a system-wide singleton. PID file is per-PPID to allow concurrent anima sessions.
shutdown_trigger: explicit: hexa session-end hook sends {"kind":"shutdown"}; worker exits cleanly within 100ms
implicit: worker is a child of hexa process group — SIGHUP/SIGTERM cascades naturally on parent exit (no orphans)
idle: worker exits after IDLE_TIMEOUT_S (default 300s) of no requests to bound resident RAM in long-lived sessions
lifecycle.session_warmup: optional pre-warm in anima_audio init: send {"kind":"ping"} immediately so first real call sees no spawn jitter

worker_crash_mid_call.detection: broken pipe on send() or readline returns empty string
worker_crash_mid_call.recovery: delete PID file, respawn worker, retry the request once (idempotent — synth has no observable state across calls; rng auto-reseeds)
worker_crash_mid_call.after_max: _run_py returns false; caller observes WAV missing and propagates empty-string sentinel as today

worker_hangs.recovery: SIGTERM worker, await 100ms, SIGKILL if still alive, respawn, retry once

worker_traceback.recovery: no respawn; return false; one bad code blob does not reset state — but DO log err for postmortem

stdin_buffer_blockage.mitigation: always flush after write; never queue >1 in-flight request in v1 (request/response strictly synchronous)

file_descriptor_exhaustion.mitigation: worker explicitly closes wave handles in finally blocks (organs already do this — verified)

pid_management.pid_file_path: /tmp/anima_audio_worker_${PPID}.pid  // PPID = the hexa process pid; per-session isolation
race_avoidance: atomic write: write to .tmp then rename
on read: kill -0 <pid> to verify worker is alive AND its /proc/<pid>/comm contains 'python' before reuse
if mismatch (pid recycled to another process), treat as stale and respawn
no flock needed — PID file is per-PPID, parallel anima sessions get distinct files
pid_management.cleanup: atexit handler in anima_audio session removes PID file; orphan files are harmless because next session uses its own PPID-suffixed path
pid_management.discoverability: anima/state/active_audio_workers.jsonl — append-only audit log (pid, ppid, start_ts, end_ts) for debugging; not load-bearing

persistent_worker.infra_complexity: moderate — adds ~80 LOC in hexa runtime + 1 new python file (~120 LOC)
persistent_worker.philosophy_compliance: PARTIAL — introduces process state across organ calls; from anima's view organs remain pure (same input WAV path → same output WAV path)
persistent_worker.risk_profile: PID leaks, zombies on hexa SIGKILL, RAM cost ~80MB resident
persistent_worker.blast_radius_if_buggy: all 49 organ call sites; mitigated by _run_py signature preservation
persistent_worker.score_overall: BEST for chained synthesis (60x speedup); PROVEN pattern (same as how IPython kernel, lsp servers, jupyter, sbt/scala daemon work)

current_spawn_per_call.score_overall: FAIL the latency budget at omega-D (1177ms chain vs 96ms target)

ctypes_shared_lib.amortization: perfect — library loaded once at hexa startup
ctypes_shared_lib.infra_complexity: HIGH — must compile a C/Rust shim exposing numpy/scipy ops; hexa runtime must learn FFI; pip wheels for numpy/scipy do NOT expose stable C API for lfilter/butter/sosfilt — would need to reimplement the DSP
ctypes_shared_lib.risk_profile: platform fragility (linux/macos/arm differ); breaks on numpy upgrade
ctypes_shared_lib.blast_radius_if_buggy: anima_audio entirely; segfaults take down hexa runtime
ctypes_shared_lib.score_overall: REJECTED — ctypes path is essentially 'rewrite the DSP' with extra steps; persistent worker dominates

pure_hexa_dsp.infra_complexity: VERY HIGH — needs hexa-native FFT, IIR, filter design (butterworth pole-placement, etc.); months of work
pure_hexa_dsp.risk_profile: math correctness regression vs reference scipy implementation
pure_hexa_dsp.score_overall: RIGHT direction long-term; OUT OF SCOPE for omega-audio-2 — explicitly noted in extended_baseline as 'large rewrite, multi-cycle implementation'

lazy_scipy_only.infra_complexity: LOW — inline 10 lines of numpy in pipe()/pipe3()
lazy_scipy_only.score_overall: COMPOSABLE — orthogonal win; recommend stacking on top of persistent worker, not as alternative

migration_phases.name: spec freeze + bench harness
actions: build a standalone benchmark: 100x vocal() calls, current vs simulated worker, on the same machine that produced 160ms baseline
migration_phases.exit_gate: bench shows persistent-worker prototype hits <15ms p95

migration_phases.name: POC on vocal() only
actions: implement _audio_worker.py with vocal-codepath only (numpy + wave, no scipy)
add _run_py_v2() in anima_audio.hexa beside _run_py — only vocal() switches over
feature flag: env var ANIMA_AUDIO_WORKER=1 enables; default off
migration_phases.rollback: unset env var; vocal() returns to spawn-per-call; zero state to clean
migration_phases.exit_gate: vocal_avg drops from 160ms to <20ms; output WAV byte-identical to baseline (md5 match required)

migration_phases.name: expand to source organs (vocal_lf, breath, tone, chirp, silence)
actions: add scipy.signal.butter+sosfilt to worker import set (covers breath)
verify: breath anomaly (1000ms — see omega_audio_extended_baseline) collapses to ~10ms — confirms cold-start hypothesis
migration_phases.rollback: per-organ flag; each organ can fall back independently
migration_phases.exit_gate: all 6 source organs match baseline WAVs bit-exact

migration_phases.name: expand to filter organs (pipe, pipe3, pipe_glide, lpf, hpf, bpf, gain, envelope, reverb, notch)
actions: add lfilter, lfiltic, fftconvolve to worker imports
rerun omega_audio_emp.hexa Ω-D — confirm chain drops from 1177ms to <30ms
rerun omega_audio_emp.hexa Ω-A/Ω-B/Ω-C/Ω-E — confirm zero quality regression
migration_phases.rollback: per-organ flag; in worst case revert to phase-2 set
migration_phases.exit_gate: Ω-D verdict flips OVER_BUDGET → PASS with margin ≥60ms

migration_phases.name: cutover — _run_py becomes worker-backed by default
actions: remove the v2 suffix; old spawn-per-call path stays as ANIMA_AUDIO_WORKER=0 escape hatch for 1 release cycle
delete ANIMA_AUDIO_WORKER=0 path after 1 month of clean operation
migration_phases.rollback: env var still works; full revert is `git revert <cutover-commit>`
migration_phases.exit_gate: 1 month of production anima sessions with zero worker-related incident reports

risks.risk: PID file leak — orphan PID files accumulate in /tmp
risks.mitigation: PPID-suffixed names age out with /tmp cleanup; atexit handler removes own file; periodic sweep job optional

risks.risk: zombie worker processes after hexa SIGKILL (no atexit run)
risks.mitigation: worker registers as child of hexa process group; SIGHUP cascades on terminal close; worker also has IDLE_TIMEOUT self-exit (300s)

risks.risk: state contamination — global mutable state in numpy/scipy persists across calls (e.g., RNG seed, np.seterr settings)
risks.mitigation: each request runs in a fresh local namespace (exec(code, {'__builtins__': __builtins__})); RNG re-seeded per request if seed param present; np.seterr restored in finally; explicit allowlist of carry-over globals (numpy, scipy, wave)

risks.risk: RAM cost ~80MB resident per anima session vs 0MB ephemeral
risks.mitigation: IDLE_TIMEOUT exits worker after 5 min of inactivity; for batch synthesis (the actual bottleneck) this is amortized over hundreds of calls — net win

risks.risk: race on concurrent anima sessions writing same PID file
risks.mitigation: PPID-suffix isolation; no shared PID file

risks.risk: non-determinism if rng auto-reseeds vs current per-call rng.default_rng() (which also auto-seeds)
risks.mitigation: behavior matches current — both use OS entropy; for repro, accept seed param in request schema (already designed)

risks.risk: stdin/stdout deadlock if worker writes >PIPE_BUF (4KB on darwin) before hexa reads
risks.mitigation: responses are tiny JSON (<256 bytes); worker stderr is consumed via separate fd; no large data flows over stdout (WAVs go to disk as today)

risks.risk: version skew — worker python and hexa-emitted code drift apart
risks.mitigation: version field in ready/handshake; mismatch → respawn with bundled worker

risks.risk: macOS App Nap / SIGSTOP on backgrounded anima session pauses worker
risks.mitigation: worker is a child of foreground hexa; inherits scheduling class; not Apple-backgrounded independently

open_questions: Q1: should phase-1 POC use base64 or length-prefix framing? recommend base64 for debuggability; revisit if p95 > 15ms
Q2: should worker pre-import every scipy.signal symbol or lazy-import on first use per organ? recommend pre-import (one-time +45ms paid at boot, not per-call)

Q3: does hexa runtime expose exec() with stdin pipe access today, or do we need a new builtin (e.g., spawn_persistent / send_stdin)? — must verify before phase-1; if not, phase-0 includes a tiny runtime patch
next_step_if_approved: POC on vocal() organ, measure roundtrip latency vs target 10ms (phase-1 exit gate)
